programmes.show_description: A look at how products are manufactured around the world. Featuring Titan Tires, espresso and how diamonds are cut. S10, Ep9
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. Featuring the complex network of Paris sewers, Beretta's and cotton manufacture. S10, Ep10
David's VW Golf GTI. Mike and Elvis help teacher David hunt down his dream car, a classic VW Golf GTI. But things get off to a rocky start when they have to scrap David's van. S2, Ep9
Mo's Porsche Cayman. Mike and Elvis help 51-year-old Mo who's been trying to save for a Porsche Cayman ever since he started working. Can the boys turn Mo's dreams into reality? S2, Ep10
The Need For Speed. Henry and Sam are in Lancashire, visiting a petrol head with a collection of vintage bikes and rare British and American four-wheelers. S2, Ep10
Bicycles And Stationary Engines. In Oxfordshire, Henry and Sam find a shed full of different items. Sam has his eye on some model trains while Henry finds a vintage bike. S2, Ep11
Follow the production process of yoghurts, candles and neon signs, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep6
Follow the production process of bottle tops, chocolate, pills and pasta, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep7
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Lucia uses an unusual painting method from Michelangelo's era in a restoration. Meanwhile, Steve and Will tackle a table with a secret cocktail compartment. S4, Ep15
Georgian Special. Drew indulges his Georgian obsession in Lincoln, where he falls for a Regency wine cooler. And a bungalow near Stockport hides a vast collection. S18, Ep7
Survivors. Drew finds rare pieces that have stood the test of time. They include an 18th century cabinet in superb original condition and a chest from the Vatican. S18, Ep8
The Gypsies strike a patch of gold but are distracted when thieves raid their nearby lease. Meanwhile, torrential rain disrupts production for the Dirt Dogs. S4, Ep4
The Victoria Diggers' million-dollar dream is nearly at breaking point. Plus, the Dirt Dogs hunt down gold thieves and the Mahoneys stumble across a mysterious murder. S4, Ep5
On a journey through Bedfordshire that includes airships and tractors, Irita splurges on fine silver while Tim goes for bargain basement. Who'll clean up at auction? S22, Ep8
In County Durham, Angus and the team clear out a huge aviation collection. Later, they help a retired policeman sell items from his four-bedroom home. S1, Ep8
Lucia uses an unusual painting method from Michelangelo's era in a restoration. Meanwhile, Steve and Will tackle a table with a secret cocktail compartment. S4, Ep15
Ploughman's. Henry and Sam rummage through a tractor enthusiasts heaven, sheds and fields full of old machines. Their attention is caught by an old vintage plough. S2, Ep12
Army Trailers And Trinkets. Henry and Sam find themselves down in the South West rummaging around another belting shed. There are so many things on offer they find it hard to choose. S2, Ep13
Stephen. Henry and Fuzz visit Stephen, a diehard biker with a shed full of rare Czech and Japanese bikes, vintage cans, and motoring curios. S6, Ep15
Graham Steeler. Henry and Fuzz head to Southend to visit Graham's shed full of bikes and muscle cars. They find a Dutton kit car and rows and rows of rare bikes. S6, Ep16
Jon Culshaw entertains Angus with hilarious impressions as they dig through clutter. Plus, actress Catherine Tyldesley auctions off her jewellery. S1, Ep2
Angus helps Claire Sweeney sell off some very glamorous items. Plus, Christopher Biggins needs to declutter - can he part with any of his beloved art collection? S1, Ep3
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep10
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep11
The Old Master And The Model Maker. Angus discovers a mysterious 17th century artwork in Scotland. Then, he heads to Peterborough to help clear the home of a keen model maker. S3, Ep11
Angus helps the ex-EastEnders star Samantha Womack shift her showbiz things, and former MP Alan Johnson parts with a chess set and much-loved guitar. S5, Ep5
Hi-Do-Hi! and panto star Jeffrey Holland bids farewell to treasures, while broadcaster Kaye Adams makes headlines with her rare Danish furniture. S5, Ep6
What is the secret of the lion dance, how are tunnels built, and what is a super sopper? Find out as more items are put under the microscope. S10, Ep11
How do they make pistachios, what is the Forbidden City, and how do they produce radars? Find out as more items are put under the microscope. S10, Ep12
How do they build wrecker trucks, how do they produce Roquefort, and how are trees transported? Find out as more items are examined. S10, Ep13
How do they produce marble, how do they make mahjong pieces, and how do they build escape slides? Find out as more items are put under the microscope. S10, Ep14
Jensen Interceptor. Mike wants a classic Jensen Interceptor. New, the car costs as much as a house, so if he does find one for his £5000, Ed might struggle to make it roadworthy. S7, Ep1
Ford Sierra Cosworth. Mike's after a Ford Sierra Cosworth, which looks like a family vehicle and runs like a race car. But with only £5000 to spend, is his plan about to stall? S7, Ep2
Ploughman's. Henry and Sam rummage through a tractor enthusiasts heaven, sheds and fields full of old machines. Their attention is caught by an old vintage plough. S2, Ep12
Army Trailers And Trinkets. Henry and Sam find themselves down in the South West rummaging around another belting shed. There are so many things on offer they find it hard to choose. S2, Ep13
Follow the production process of honey, fibre optics and bricks, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep8
Follow the production process of carpets, drinking water and acoustic guitars, and find out how each of these items is made. S1, Ep9
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Matt has to use the utmost precision when restoring some stunning stained glass. Meanwhile, Jay and Dom work on an old tram seat that has been in a family for decades. S4, Ep16
Ireland Special. Exploring Ireland's thriving antiques world, Drew and new buying partner Alister uncover treasures in private homes and showrooms across the country. S18, Ep9
Scotland Special. Drew gets an insider's tour of Scotland with local dealer, Viki Knott. He discovers a Mackintosh-influenced chair and some sinister 1960s Americana. S18, Ep10
The Victoria Diggers' desperately fight for survival, but they may have stumbled across a monster nugget. The Gold Gypsies fend off deadly snakes during their search. S4, Ep6
The Gold Gypsies hunt for nuggets in ancient mine shafts and Greg discovers a rare meteorite. The Mahoneys chase for more gold using Ted's granddad's treasure map. S4, Ep7
Tim takes up West Country skittles while Irita spins a good sailing yarn at an historic Somerset twineworks. Both buy pig themed antiques - who trots home the victor? S22, Ep9
Angus is in Fife to dig through three generations of family clutter. They uncover a rocking horse and a collection of rare Beatrix Potter figurines. S1, Ep9
Matt has to use the utmost precision when restoring some stunning stained glass. Meanwhile, Jay and Dom work on an old tram seat that has been in a family for decades. S4, Ep16
Steam, Bicycles And Metal Rolling. In Cornwall, Henry and Sam find some very rare early-1900s steam-powered cars. If they're very lucky, the duo might even get to ride in one! S2, Ep14
Signs And Wonders. In Somerset, Henry and Sam visit a man with an amazing collection of motorbikes. He has some rare finds, but are they too expensive for the guys? S2, Ep15
Will Ralph. Henry and Fuzz visit a farm near Colchester with vintage tractors galore. The boys come away with a classic Fordson and some old traffic lights. S6, Ep17
Robin Burford. The boys visit Rob, who has an impressive collection of vintage British bikes. They find a BSA Bantam and a classic 1930's Austin Ten automobile. S6, Ep18
Angus visits Debbie McGee and finds a treasure trove of magical items, but what is she willing to sell? And Rav Wilding is keen to sell some family heirlooms. S1, Ep4
Angus helps Wayne Sleep shift a wardrobe of costumes worn over his decades-spanning career. Plus, Jodie Kidd reveals a stable full of family treasures. S1, Ep5
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep12
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit. S25, Ep13
Tim And Camilla / Digger. Henry and Fuzz are down on the farm in Oxfordshire. They unearth mowers and tractors in Tim and Camilla's barns but end up buying something very different. S5, Ep5
Taff / Chopper. Henry and Fuzz motor over to Taff's place in Lincoln. His extensive collection of Easy Rider-style chopper motorbikes includes an engraved 1970s Yamaha. S5, Ep6
Last Chance Dig. The Blacklighters have just one week left to strike opal before the summer heat shuts them down. The Bushmen scramble to beat mechanical failures. S8, Ep19
Old Mates And Motor-Racing. Richard gets into hot water as he tries to upgrade his racing licence and the Cogs head to the Isle of Man TT to help launch a new British motorbike. S5, Ep6
